flows: user -> admin — type=association; label="login"; explanation="user logs in to admin system"
user -> product — type=association; label="add"; explanation="user adds a product to their cart"
user -> cart — type=association; label="add"; explanation="user adds an item to their cart"
admin -> product — type=association; label="add"; explanation="admin adds a product to the system"
admin -> cart — type=association; label="add"; explanation="admin adds an item to their cart"
user -> cart — type=association; label="remove"; explanation="user removes an item from their cart"
admin -> cart — type=association; label="remove"; explanation="admin removes an item from their cart"
flow 1: user -> admin -> cart
user logs in
user adds a product to their cart
user logs out
the diagram models a simple e-commerce system where users can log in, add products to their cart, and remove items from their cart.
the main flows show the interactions between the user and the admin system, including login, adding products to the cart, and removing items from the cart.
the system allows for the addition and removal of items from the cart, indicating that the system supports both incremental and decremental operations.
guidelines by diagram type (keep it generic):
for class diagrams:
list each class with its attributes and operations.
in the component bullet, list all associations, aggregations, and inheritances.
for activity diagrams:
list each activity with its attributes and operations.
in the component bullet, list all activities and operations.
for sequence diagrams:
list each sequence with its attributes and operations.

validations: user -> admin — type=message; label="login"; explanation="user sends login request to admin system"
admin -> user — type=message; label="login"; explanation="admin sends login response to user"
user -> cart — type=message; label="add"; explanation="user sends add request to cart"
cart -> user — type=message; label="add"; explanation="cart sends add response to user"
user -> cart — type=message; label="remove"; explanation="user sends remove request to cart"
cart -> user — type=message; label="remove"; explanation="cart sends remove response to user"
